content.p: The terms “clean hydrogen” and “hydrogen” mean hydrogen produced in compliance with the greenhouse gas emissions standard established under section 16166(a) of this title, including production from any fuel source.

The term “fuel cell” means a device that directly converts the chemical energy of a fuel, which is supplied from an external source, and an oxidant into electricity by electrochemical processes occurring at separate electrodes in the device.

paragraph.heading: Heavy-duty vehicle
paragraph.chapeau: The term “heavy-duty vehicle” means a motor vehicle that—

subparagraph.content: is rated at more than 8,500 pounds gross vehicle weight;
has a curb weight of more than 6,000 pounds; or
has a basic vehicle frontal area in excess of 45 square feet.

content.p: The term “infrastructure” means the equipment, systems, or facilities used to produce, distribute, deliver, or store hydrogen (except for onboard storage).
The term “light-duty vehicle” means a motor vehicle that is rated at 8,500 or less pounds gross vehicle weight.

paragraph.heading: Portable; storage
paragraph.chapeau: The terms “portable” and “storage”, when used in reference to a fuel cell, include—

content.p: The term “Task Force” means the Hydrogen and Fuel Cell Technical Task Force established under section 16155 of this title.
The term “Technical Advisory Committee” means the independent Technical Advisory Committee established under section 16156 of this title.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 2021—Pars. (1) to (4). Pub. L. 117–58, § 40312(2), (3), added par. (1) and redesignated former pars. (1) to (3) as (2) to (4), respectively. Former par. (4) redesignated (5).
Par. (5). Pub. L. 117–58, § 40312(2), redesignated par. (4) as (5). Former par. (5) redesignated (6).

Pub. L. 117–58, § 40312(1), substituted “Portable; storage” for “Stationary; portable” in heading and “The terms ‘portable’ and ‘storage’, when” for “The terms ‘stationary’ and ‘portable’, when” in introductory provisions.
Pars. (6) to (8). Pub. L. 117–58, § 40312(2), redesignated pars. (5) to (7) as (6) to (8), respectively.

note.heading: Wage Rate Requirements
note.p: For provisions relating to rates of wages to be paid to laborers and mechanics on projects for construction, alteration, or repair work funded under div. D or an amendment by div. D of Pub. L. 117–58, including authority of Secretary of Labor, see section 18851 of this title.
